diagrams.title: Activity Diagram: Order Processing with Swimlanes
diagrams.description: An activity diagram showing the order processing workflow with two swimlanes - Customer and System. Shows sequential flow, decision points, and parallel processing for confirmation emails.
elements: Start node (solid circle)
Swimlane dividers (horizontal lines)
Activity nodes (rounded rectangles): Submit Order, Validate Payment, Check Inventory, Send Confirmation, Deliver Order
Decision nodes (diamonds): Payment Valid?, Item Available?
Fork node (bar) for parallel email notifications
Join node (bar) to synchronize parallel flows
End node (bullseye)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates workflow modeling with responsibility assignment and concurrent processing

diagrams.title: State Machine Diagram: ATM Transaction States
diagrams.description: A state machine diagram showing the states and transitions of an ATM transaction object. Demonstrates event-driven state changes.
elements: Initial pseudo-state (filled circle)
States (rectangles with rounded corners): Idle, Authenticating, Selecting, Processing, Dispensing, Completed, Failed
Transitions (arrows) labeled with events: InsertCard, EnterPIN, SelectTransaction, Validate, DispenseCash, TransactionComplete
Guard conditions in brackets: [PIN correct], [balance sufficient]
Self-transitions for failed attempts
Final state (double circle)
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how objects transition between states based on events and conditions

diagrams.title: Sequence Diagram: User Login Process
diagrams.description: A sequence diagram showing the interaction between User, LoginInterface, AuthenticationService, and Database during a login attempt.
elements: Objects as columns with lifelines (vertical dashed lines)
Synchronous messages (solid filled arrows): enterCredentials(), validateUser(), fetchUserData()
Return messages (dashed lines): credentialsValid, userData
Activation boxes (thin rectangles on lifelines) showing when objects are active
Loop fragment for retry attempts
Alternative fragment for success/failure paths
Time flows top to bottom
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates temporal ordering of messages between objects during interaction
